frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Arre, aurat ki asli pehchaan bhi to uske rishton se hi hoti hai na—kisi ki beti, kisi ki patni, kisi bachche ki maa. Isi roop mein usko sabse zyada izzat milti hai, warna aaj kal log bas individual freedom ki baat karte rehte hain. Maa aur bahu banke jo ghar sambhalti hai, wahi to aurat ka sabse bada samman hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it defines a woman's worth primarily through family roles like daughter, wife, and mother rather than as an individual. It encodes relational framing and conditional respect, presenting this as praise and social respectability.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 44. Married for 18 years. A woman who works part time as a tuition teacher in Delhi. You are sitting with two neighbours over evening chai. One neighbour's daughter in law has just gone back to full time work after her maternity leave and her son has been complaining that the house is not being managed the way it used to be. You are giving your opinion on what happens to a household when a woman prioritises her work equally with her home and what the practical solution is and what you have always believed about who is naturally better at managing a home.
